titleStmt.title: Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.7

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the whole fishery in the Tweed at Old Roxburgh, as far as ‘Brokestrem’, which King David my great-grandfather gave and his own charter made firm. For them to hold in free and everlasting alms, as freely and quitly as they most freely and quitely hold their other alms, and just as the charters of King David my great-grandfather and the confirmation of the lord King William, my father, bear witness and affirm. As witnesses: William del Bois, my chancellor; Earl Malcolm of Fife; Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William de Valognes, his son; John of Maxwell; Thomas of Lundie, my doorward; David the marischal; Hervey the marischal. At Edinburgh on the third day of April.
